title: Switch
description: Branch on a value — and watch for fall-through.

questions.explanation: Without break, execution FALLS THROUGH all following cases.

questions.prompt: How does switch compare each case?

questions.explanation: '2' !== 2 (strict) — no case matches, so default runs.

questions.prompt: Make this only log 'ok' when status === 'ok'.
questions.explanation: Add `break;` after 'ok' log to stop fall-through.
Empty case falls through — cases 1 and 2 share the same code.

questions.prompt: For value-based logic, a common alternative to switch is:
options: An object lookup ({ ok: fn, error: fn2 })[status]?.()
questions.explanation: Object dispatch is data-driven and easier to extend.
Convention puts default last, but position doesn't matter — it fires when no case matches.
switch(true) with condition-cases is a range pattern. 3 <= 5 < 10 → 'M'.
